focus: maintainability: integration contract: convergence.cjs <-> upsert.cjs <-> reduce-state, fanout-run <-> fanout-pool <-> fanout-salvage <-> fanout-merge

title: Exit-zero salvage misses are reported as fulfilled lineages
detail: fanout-run records the salvage result but rejects only on timeout or non-zero subprocess exit. If a child exits 0 while required iteration markdown is missing and stdout is unrecoverable, fanout-salvage increments failed and writes a failed marker, yet fanout-run returns a fulfilled result. The failure classifier already has salvage_miss semantics, but they are only reached after a throw.
claim: A lineage with unrecoverable missing iteration artifacts can be counted as successful when the subprocess exits 0, silently losing required review output.
counterevidenceSought: Checked fanout-run for any post-salvage guard on salvage.failed and checked the pool classifier/tests for salvage_miss handling; the classifier exists but is only used for rejected worker errors.
alternativeExplanation: The placeholder file may have been intended to let downstream stuck recovery proceed, but the deep-review contract requires dispatch scripts to fail when required iteration artifacts are missing, and fanout-run's summary currently reports the lineage as fulfilled.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the workflow intentionally treats exit-zero salvage placeholders as valid successful lineage output and a later mandatory gate fails the run before merge/synthesis.
suggestedFix: After runSalvageSweep, throw a retryable lineage error when salvage.failed > 0, attach the salvage summary, and add an exit-zero/unrecoverable-salvage regression test.

title: Reducer can drop findings when the delta file lacks per-finding rows
detail: reduce-state builds findings from separate type=finding delta rows and markdown parsing, while post-dispatch validation only requires findingDetails to be an array and the delta file to contain an iteration row. A nonzero-finding iteration with findingDetails in the canonical record but no per-finding delta lines can validate and then reduce to an empty openFindings registry.
claim: The validator/reducer contract allows structured finding data to pass validation but be ignored by the registry builder, which can produce a false PASS or undercount active findings.
counterevidenceSought: Looked for validation that delta type=finding rows match findingsCount/findingDetails and for reducer fallback from iteration findingDetails; only array-shape validation and iteration-record presence were found.
alternativeExplanation: Agents are instructed to emit per-finding delta rows, so well-behaved iterations avoid the bug; the defect is that the machine gate does not enforce that contract and the reducer ignores the redundant structured data it already validates.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if validateIterationOutputs has another active path that compares findingDetails/findingsCount to type=finding rows before reduce-state runs, or if generated agents cannot produce a delta file without those rows.
suggestedFix: Make post-dispatch validation fail when findingsCount or findingDetails is nonzero but matching type=finding rows are missing, or make reduce-state convert findingDetails into findings as a structured fallback.

title: Pool abort-requeue lag action is not reachable from fanout-run
detail: fanout-pool implements lagCeilingAction=abort-requeue and has tests for it, but fanout-run passes only lagCeilingMs into runCappedPool and the fanout config schema exposes no lagCeilingAction field. The production runner therefore cannot activate the tested abort/requeue path.
claim: The pool API and runner/config contract have drifted: abort-requeue behavior is tested in isolation but dead through the real fanout-run entrypoint.
counterevidenceSought: Checked fanoutConfigSchema and fanout-run's runCappedPool call for a lagCeilingAction passthrough; only lagCeilingMs is wired.
alternativeExplanation: Abort-requeue may be reserved for direct module consumers rather than CLI fanout; if so, the public runner contract should say the CLI only warns on lag ceiling.
downgradeTrigger: Drop if abort-requeue is intentionally private test-only scaffolding or another wrapper passes lagCeilingAction directly to runCappedPool outside fanout-run.
suggestedFix: Either add lagCeilingAction to fanoutConfigSchema and pass it through from fanout-run, or remove/de-scope the abort-requeue branch from production-facing pool tests and docs until wired.
